# Open Science Pillars Tutorials {.unnumbered}

Hands-on walkthroughs for AI-assisted open science with the Open
Science Pillars plugins, on Claude Code, Cowork, and Claude Science
(the three "surfaces"). Each tutorial header records which surfaces it
has been verified on. New to a term? See the
[glossary](https://github.com/open-science-pillars/marketplace/blob/main/GLOSSARY.md).

- **Tutorial 1, Getting Started (~10 min):** install core, orient,
  quality-control a test dataset, and produce an anomaly analysis with
  the guardrails visible.
- **Tutorial 2, ECCO Heat Transport (~20 min):** real PO.DAAC data
  through the volume gate, Atlantic meridional heat transport (MHT) at 26.5N with
  honest uncertainty framing, a report with provenance, and a SWOT
  swath scene with the orbit-phase lesson.
- **Tutorial 3, Build a Domain Plugin (~30 min):** scaffold from the
  template to an installed plugin: one gated skill, one evidence-linked
  concept, one green golden notebook, with the arset-style template for
  the teaching variant.

**No-install demo:** the [browser-runnable MHT companion](demo/wasm/index.html)
tells the 2010 AMOC-minimum story (the Atlantic overturning circulation at a low) with the build's verified numbers,
running in the page (WASM, so nothing to set up) (source: `demo/mht_wasm_demo.py`).

Tutorials teach; the plugins' verification notebooks prove; the
knowledge bundles remember. The three layers are inspectable in the
[organization repositories](https://github.com/open-science-pillars).
